title: Connection Factorization in Constrained Quantum Mechanics
abstract: We investigate constrained quantum motion on curves and surfaces using connection factorization methods. We show that Laplace operators admit an exact half-connection factorization generated by connection one-forms. The first-order part of the Laplacian is identified with the Darboux rotational connection of the orthogonal frame. Elimination of this rotational connection naturally produces quadratic geometric invariants analogous to supersymmetric Riccati potentials. Using orthonormal moving frames, we derive effective geometric contributions for planar curves, spatial curves, and embedded surfaces. We further analyze Dirac reductions using structure equations and show that for Fermi-type reductions the scalar Jensen--Koppe--da Costa contribution is cancelled in the reduced Dirac sector, leaving a residual first-order spinorial derivative structure. The resulting framework suggests the existence of hidden nilpotent covariant differential complexes naturally generated by geometric connection structures.
summary: This work develops a geometric framework to analyze quantum mechanics constrained to curved spaces using connection factorization. It demonstrates that the Laplacian operator can be factored using geometric connections, and it finds a significant cancellation of geometric terms for Dirac fermions under specific geometric conditions. This suggests the existence of underlying nilpotent differential complexes governing the quantum dynamics.
main_problem: The paper investigates the mathematical structure of quantum motion when constrained to lower-dimensional manifolds like curves and surfaces.
main_result: It shows that the Laplace operator admits an exact half-connection factorization, and for Dirac fermions in Fermi-type reductions, the standard scalar geometric contribution cancels out, leaving a residual spinorial structure.
method: The analysis heavily relies on differential geometry tools, including orthonormal moving frames, structure equations, and connection factorization methods applied to the Laplacian and Dirac operators.
model_or_system: The model involves quantum particles whose dynamics are restricted to curved spaces (curves, planar nets, surfaces). The mathematical machinery uses geometric objects like connection one-forms and curvature tensors.
key_observables: Laplace operators, quadratic geometric invariants (analogous to supersymmetric Riccati potentials), and the residual first-order spinorial derivative structure.
important_parameters: Curvatures (mean $H$, Gaussian $K$), connection coefficients ($k_1, k_2$), and the scalar Jensen–Koppe–da Costa contribution ($V_{dC}$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the use of orthonormal moving frames and highlights that the cancellation of the scalar geometric contribution is specific to Fermi-type geometry.
paper_structure: The paper develops the framework by first analyzing the Laplacian factorization on general manifolds, then specializing to planar orthogonal nets using structure equations, and finally examining the implications for Dirac operators and the resulting nilpotent covariant differential complexes.
why_interesting: The deep connection between geometric constraints (curvature, connection forms) and the fundamental structure of quantum operators (Laplacian, Dirac) provides powerful mathematical tools applicable to quantum systems in curved backgrounds, which is a key area in theoretical physics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
